name: Customer Service & Support
description: You've watched a chatbot completely butcher a conversation you'd have handled in 30 seconds. That instinct for when things go wrong is exactly what AI safety evaluation needs.
trajectory: Spotting when a conversation is going wrong translates directly to AI evaluation and red-teaming — testing AI systems for failures.
De-escalation and reading emotional tone maps to human oversight design — ensuring humans can intervene when AI fails.
Knowing when a script makes things worse is failure mode identification — a core AI safety skill.
Frontline experience with real users provides perspectives that formal AI evaluations systematically miss.

projects.title: Red-team AI customer service bots
projects.description: Test AI chatbots with your hardest real-world customer scenarios. Document where they fail and how a human would have handled it differently.

projects.title: Write a failure taxonomy for AI support
projects.description: Catalog the types of failures AI customer service systems make that experienced support workers catch instantly.

projects.title: Design human oversight protocols for AI support
projects.description: Create guidelines for when and how human agents should intervene in AI-handled customer interactions.
